what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry for adjustable character-trait 'dials'. It scans a dials/ directory for .py modules, validates each module-level DIAL dict against a required schema, and exposes lookup and iteration helpers for the validated rows.
observations: Each dial definition file must declare a module-level DIAL dict.
Required row fields are id, label, governs, positions, and default.
The dial id must match the file stem, and the default must be one of the named positions.
positions is an ordered low-to-high list of {name, meaning} dicts with unique names.
Discovery is repo-root-anchored via nested os.path.dirname calls, not cwd.
Validation is fail-loud with ValueError; invalid rows are rejected rather than skipped.
The file only handles static dial definitions; values, overrides, and condition evaluation live elsewhere.
The class provides rows, get, position_names, __contains__, and __iter__ accessors.
standouts: Strict, explicit validation of the DIAL row schema before registration.
Repo-root-anchored discovery to avoid silent empty results when cwd differs from root.
Honest documentation that condition-scoped overrides are stored and validated but not yet evaluated.
conventions: File discovery via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location and exec_module.
Module-level constant (DIAL) as the exported contract.
Fail-fast validation with descriptive ValueError messages.
Minimal type hints and plain docstrings explaining design rationale.
concerns: Duplicate dial ids across multiple discovery directories would silently overwrite earlier rows.
Imported dial modules are executed but not removed from sys.modules or isolated.
No validation that governs is a list/string or that meanings are non-empty.
No reload/caching semantics or thread-safety considerations are visible.
notes: A fail-loud, file-discovered registry that loads and validates static DIAL definitions from a dials/ directory for runtime consumers.
questions: Where exactly are the dials/ definition files expected to reside in a deployed system?
Which component consumes this registry and how does it map governs strings to behavior?
How are dial values and condition-scoped overrides persisted, validated, and later evaluated?
What is the concrete shape of the rules-engine when clause mentioned for overrides?
Is there a Suite.set_dial / MCP dials tool implementation elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches what the file does: it discovers and validates static dial definitions and exposes them through a simple API.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud validation to keep the registry trustworthy.
Separation of dial definition from dial value/evaluation.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make character traits adjustable, named, ordered knobs that are defined as data files and consumed by runtime components through a validated catalog.
novelty: Treats adjustable character traits as ordered named positions in file-discovered registry rows, and explicitly acknowledges condition-scoped overrides as stored-but-not-yet-evaluated data.
contribution: A validated, file-discovered registry abstraction for dial definitions, with a clear row schema and a small lookup/iteration API that a fused system can reuse.
summary_for_embedding: A Python registry class that discovers and validates module-level DIAL definition dicts from a dials/ directory and exposes row lookup and position-name helpers.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a fail-loud, data-driven catalog of adjustable character-trait dials that other runtime components can read and apply.

symbols.does: Defines the file-discovered dial registry and its validation/access API.
symbols.description: A registry class that scans a dials/ directory, imports each .py dial definition, validates the module-level DIAL dict, and stores validated rows for later lookup.

symbols.does: Initializes an empty internal row dictionary.
symbols.description: Creates the registry instance with an empty _rows mapping before discovery is run.

symbols.does: Scans configured directories, imports dial modules, validates DIAL rows, and populates the registry.
symbols.description: Iterates over sorted .py files in the given directories, executes each module, validates required fields, id/stem match, positions schema, unique names, and default membership, then stores the row.

symbols.does: Returns a list of all validated dial rows.
symbols.description: Provides a snapshot of the currently registered DIAL dicts as a list.

symbols.does: Retrieves a single dial row by id, raising KeyError if unknown.
symbols.description: Looks up a validated DIAL dict by its id and fails loudly with the list of registered ids when missing.

symbols.does: Returns the ordered list of position names for a dial.
symbols.description: Convenience accessor that extracts just the name field from each position of the specified dial.

symbols.does: Supports membership testing for dial ids.
symbols.description: Allows id in registry checks against the internal _rows mapping.

symbols.does: Supports iteration over registered dial ids.
symbols.description: Yields the keys of the internal _rows mapping so callers can iterate dial ids.
